ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
description: An initiative by the government of India in which all farmers will get up to ₹6,000 per year as minimum income support.
eligibility_text: All landholding farmers' families, which have cultivable landholding in their names are eligible. Excludes institutional landholders, farmer families holding constitutional posts, serving or retired officers, professionals (doctors, engineers etc.), and pensioners with monthly pension > ₹10,000.
benefits_text: Financial benefit of ₹6,000 per year per family, payable in three equal installments of ₹2,000 each, every four months.
application_process_text: Farmers can apply online via the PM-KISAN portal or through common service centers (CSCs) and local patwaris/revenue officials.

description: A rural housing program providing a pucca house with basic amenities to all houseless families and those living in kutcha and dilapidated houses.
eligibility_text: Identified strictly based on housing deprivation parameters in the SECC 2011 data, subject to exclusion criteria. Must not own a pucca house in the country.
benefits_text: Financial assistance of ₹1.20 Lakh in plains and ₹1.30 Lakh in hilly, difficult areas, and IAP districts. Additionally, 90-95 days of unskilled labor wage under MGNREGA and ₹12,000 for toilet construction.
application_process_text: Beneficiaries are automatically selected based on SECC 2011 data. Gram Sabhas verify the list.

ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
description: To address the housing shortage among the urban poor including the EWS/LIG/MIG categories by ensuring a pucca house to all eligible urban households.
eligibility_text: Applicant must not own a pucca house anywhere in India. Income categories: EWS (Up to ₹3 Lakh/yr), LIG (₹3-6 Lakh/yr), MIG-I (₹6-12 Lakh/yr), MIG-II (₹12-18 Lakh/yr).
benefits_text: Credit Linked Subsidy up to ₹2.67 Lakhs on home loans, or assistance for construction/enhancement of houses.
application_process_text: Can apply online through the PMAY(U) MIS portal or via Common Service Centres (CSCs). Registration via banks for Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme (CLSS).

scheme_name: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY)
ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
description: World's largest health insurance scheme fully financed by the government, providing a cover of ₹5 lakhs per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization.
eligibility_text: Families identified based on the deprivation and occupational criteria of the Socio-Economic Caste Census 2011 (SECC 2011) for rural and urban areas respectively.
benefits_text: Health cover of ₹5 lakhs per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization across public and private empanelled hospitals in India. Cashless and paperless access.
application_process_text: No formal enrollment process needed. Eligible families receive a health card automatically or can generate it at empanelled hospitals/CSCs by verifying their identity.

ministry: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
description: Provides deposit-free LPG connections to women from below poverty line (BPL) households to ensure smokeless kitchens.
eligibility_text: Adult woman belonging to a poor household not possessing an LPG connection. Covers SC/ST, PMAY-G, AAY, SECC households, etc.
benefits_text: Deposit-free LPG connection including free first refill and hotplate (stove). Financial support of ₹1600 for a 14.2 kg cylinder.
application_process_text: Apply online at pmuy.gov.in or submit an application physically at the nearest LPG distributorship.

documents_needed: Aadhaar Card or other OVD (Officially Valid Document)
description: National Mission for Financial Inclusion to ensure access to financial services, namely, basic savings & deposit accounts, remittance, credit, insurance, pension in an affordable manner.
eligibility_text: Any Indian citizen over 10 years of age who does not have any other bank account.
benefits_text: Zero-balance Savings Account, RuPay Debit card with ₹2 lakh accident insurance cover, and an overdraft facility of up to ₹10,000 for eligible account holders.
application_process_text: Visit any bank branch or Bank Mitra (Business Correspondent) outlet to open an account by submitting the required KYC documents.

scheme_name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS) - NSAP
description: A non-contributory old age pension scheme for the BPL elderly.
eligibility_text: Must be 60 years or older and belong to a household below the poverty line (BPL) according to criteria prescribed by the Government of India.
benefits_text: Pension of ₹200 per month for ages 60-79, and ₹500 per month for those aged 80 and above. States usually add their own contribution.
application_process_text: Applications are submitted to the Gram Panchayat / Municipal officials, accompanied by BPL verification.

scheme_name: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS) - NSAP
description: A non-contributory widow pension scheme for BPL widows.
eligibility_text: Widows aged 40 to 79 years belonging to BPL households.
benefits_text: Pension of ₹300 per month. After age 80, they transition to IGNOAPS to receive ₹500 per month.
application_process_text: Apply via Gram Panchayat / Municipal bodies with husband's death certificate and BPL verification.

scheme_name: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (IGNDPS) - NSAP
description: Pension for persons with severe or multiple disabilities from BPL families.
eligibility_text: Must be aged 18-79 years, belong to a BPL family, and have severe or multiple disabilities (80% and above).
benefits_text: Pension of ₹300 per month. After age 80, transition to IGNOAPS for ₹500 per month.
application_process_text: Apply locally with a certified disability certificate.

description: Lump sum assistance on the death of a primary breadwinner in a BPL family.
eligibility_text: A BPL household where the primary breadwinner (aged 18-59) has died.
benefits_text: One-time lump sum assistance of ₹20,000 to the bereaved household.
application_process_text: Submit application to local administration (Tehsildar / BDO) soon after the death.

description: Provides food security to meet the requirement of those senior citizens who, though eligible, have remained uncovered under IGNOAPS.
eligibility_text: Age 65+ years, destitute, must be eligible for IGNOAPS but practically NOT receiving any pension.
benefits_text: 10 Kgs of food grains (wheat or rice) given free of cost per month per beneficiary.
application_process_text: Apply to local Gram Panchayat / Municipal Authorities with declaration of zero pension.

scheme_name: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA)
description: Enhances livelihood security in rural areas by providing at least 100 days of guaranteed wage employment in a financial year to every household whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled manual work.
eligibility_text: Any Indian citizen above 18 years living in a rural area and willing to do unskilled manual work.
benefits_text: Guaranteed 100 days of employment in a year. Provision of minimum wages set by the state. Unemployment allowance if work is not provided within 15 days.
application_process_text: Register at the local Gram Panchayat to obtain a 'Job Card' by applying orally or in writing.

ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
description: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme for Small and Marginal Farmers (SMF) to provide a social security net as they grow old.
eligibility_text: Small and Marginal Farmers holding cultivable land up to 2 hectares. Entry age between 18 to 40 years. Must not be covered under other statutory social security schemes like NPS, ESIC, EPFO etc.
benefits_text: Assured monthly pension of ₹3,000 upon attaining the age of 60 years. In case of death, the spouse receives 50% as family pension.
application_process_text: Apply via Common Service Centres (CSCs) or self-enrollment on the maandhan.in portal. Contributions range from ₹55 to ₹200 per month.

scheme_name: PM Street Vendor's AtmaNirbhar Nidhi (PM SVANidhi)
ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
description: A special micro-credit facility to provide affordable loans to street vendors to resume their livelihoods.
eligibility_text: Street vendors engaged in vending in urban areas as on or before March 24, 2020. Having Certificate of Vending / Identity Card issued by Urban Local Bodies.
benefits_text: Working capital loan up to ₹10,000 initially. Enhanced limit of ₹20,000 and ₹50,000 on timely repayment of earlier loans. 7% interest subsidy on prompt repayment.
application_process_text: Apply online via the PM SVANidhi portal or visit a nearby banking agent / CSC center.

description: Micro Units Development and Refinance Agency Ltd. [MUDRA] scheme to provide loans up to 10 lakhs to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises. 'Shishu' covers loans up to ₹50,000.
eligibility_text: Any Indian citizen who has a valid business plan for a non-farm sector income generating activity. Specifically targeting startups and first-time entrepreneurs.
benefits_text: Collateral-free loan up to ₹50,000 to start or expand a micro business.
application_process_text: Apply directly at authorized banks, NBFCs, MFIs, or online through the Udyamimitra portal.

description: MUDRA loan 'Kishore' covers loans above ₹50,000 and up to ₹5 Lakh.
eligibility_text: For entrepreneurs who have started a business and are looking to expand it.
benefits_text: Collateral-free loan from ₹50,001 to ₹5,00,000 for business expansion.
application_process_text: Apply through lending institutions (banks, NBFCs) or the Udyamimitra portal.

description: MUDRA loan 'Tarun' covers loans above ₹5 Lakh and up to ₹10 Lakh.
eligibility_text: Established businesses that need substantial working capital or fixed asset funding to grow.
benefits_text: Collateral-free loan from ₹5,00,001 to ₹10,00,000.
application_process_text: Submit a detailed project report and application to lending institutions like commercial banks.

documents_needed: Identity and Address proof of guardian/parent
description: A Govt of India backed savings scheme targeted at the parents of girl children, under 'Beti Bachao, Beti Padhao' campaign.
eligibility_text: The account can be opened in the name of a girl child anytime before she attains the age of 10 years. Max 2 accounts per family (3 in case of twins).
benefits_text: Highly attractive interest rate (currently approx 8.2%), tax benefits under 80C, maturity after 21 years or marriage of the girl after 18.
application_process_text: Visit any Post office or authorized commercial banks to open the account with a minimum deposit of ₹250.

ministry: Ministry of Finance / Ministry of Agriculture
description: Provides adequate and timely credit support from the banking system to farmers for their cultivation and agriculture-related needs.
eligibility_text: All farmers - individuals/joint borrowers who are owner cultivators, tenant farmers, Oral lessees & Sharecroppers, SHGs or Joint Liability Groups of farmers.
benefits_text: Credit limit to meet agricultural expenses, post-harvest expenses, etc., at concessional interest rates (approx 4% with subvention). Also covers animal husbandry and fisheries.
application_process_text: Apply directly at any commercial bank, Regional Rural Bank or Cooperative Bank.

ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
description: Comprehensive crop insurance scheme protecting farmers against unforeseen events affecting crop yields.
eligibility_text: All farmers growing notified crops in a notified area during the season who have an insurable interest in the crop.
benefits_text: Very low premium paid by farmers (2% for Kharif, 1.5% for Rabi, 5% for commercial/horticulture). Financial support in case of crop failure due to natural calamities.
application_process_text: Registration via banks, common service centers (CSCs), or the PMFBY online portal before the specified cutoff dates for the season.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana (PMGKAY)
ministry: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution
description: A food security welfare scheme aimed at feeding the poorest citizens of India by providing free food grains.
eligibility_text: Families holding Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) and Priority Household (PHH) ration cards under the National Food Security Act (NFSA).
benefits_text: Free extra 5 kg of food grains (rice or wheat) per month per person, in addition to the monthly subsidized entitlements under NFSA.
application_process_text: Automatically provided to eligible ration card holders at the Fair Price Shops (FPS) using POS machines.

description: The Pradhan Mantri Vaya Vandana Yojana (PMVVY) is a vital initiative by the Government of India designed to uplift specific demographics through sustained economic, social, or foundational support.
eligibility_text: Indian citizens fulfilling the criteria outlined under the Pradhan Mantri Vaya Vandana Yojana (PMVVY) guidelines are eligible to apply.
benefits_text: Participants of the Pradhan Mantri Vaya Vandana Yojana (PMVVY) receive targeted benefits, financial assistance, or subsidized services as demarcated by the national budget.
application_process_text: Prospective applicants can submit their details through the official government portal or registered Common Service Centres (CSC) nationwide.
